anchored_threads.anchor_evidence: BG 2.22 is the canonical illustration of the first thread: separate what is invariant from what is current implementation. The wardrobe simile is the chapter's most quotable rendering of architectural-invariants vs. churning-implementations. The chapter thesis explicitly names this verse-block as load-bearing for the engineering layer: 'the interface survives even when the implementation churns.' The engineering analog matches the thesis verbatim in framing.

progression_issue.explanation: Position 22 is the simile-illustration of the imperishable doctrine established in 2.20-2.21 and elaborated in 2.23-2.25. The engineering layer correctly stays at the first-principles register and does not preempt the karma-yoga block (2.31+) by introducing shipping-discipline language. The simile is treated as illustration, not as conclusion.

verdict_reason: BG 2.22 is the chapter's canonical body-as-clothing simile. The engineering layer is sanctioned by the chapter thesis ('the spec survives even when the code rewrites'). The verse's hard-edge — 'the atman survives change' should NOT be flattened into 'embrace change' — is honoured by the falsifiability and counter-example carve-outs. PASS at the chapter's calibration standard.
